rationale: The citing paper uses Slotegraaf, Moorman, and Inman (2003) in core hypothesis framing, explicitly drawing on its finding that higher-equity brands get stronger immediate marketing-mix returns. It also cites that paper in the methods section as precedent for the sequential hierarchical regression approach used to explain estimated long-term promotional effects. This is more than a passing literature mention because the citation informs both the conceptual setup and empirical design.
relation_description: The 2008 study extends Slotegraaf, Moorman, and Inman (2003) by moving from immediate promotional returns to cumulative and permanent (long-term) effects. It carries forward the resource-contingent logic and related sequential modeling approach to test how brand equity and product introductions condition long-term promotion effectiveness.
